Case.Subject: Billing dispute - overcharged $89.99, plan is $49.99
Case.Description: Customer charged $89.99 on 2026-05-26; expected $49.99. Second occurrence. Requests fix + refund.

records.Subject: Billing dispute - overcharged
records.TextBody: I was charged $89.99 on May 26 but my plan is supposed to be $49.99/month. This is the second time this has happened. Please fix this and refund the difference.

records.Subject: RE: Billing dispute - overcharged
records.TextBody: Hi Maria, I'm sorry about that. I can see the $89.99 charge. It looks like an add-on was applied in error. I'll remove it going forward and issue a $40 credit. You should see it within 3-5 business days.

records.Subject: RE: Billing dispute - overcharged
records.TextBody: Thank you. Also, can someone call me to confirm? I'd rather not keep emailing back and forth.

records.Subject: RE: Billing dispute - overcharged
records.TextBody: Absolutely. I'll have someone from our billing team call you tomorrow (May 29) between 1-3 PM your local time.

records.Subject: RE: Billing dispute - overcharged
records.TextBody: No one ever called, and I still don't see the $40 credit on my account. It's been almost a week.

records.Subject: RE: Billing dispute - overcharged
records.TextBody: Hi Maria, apologies for the delay. I'm checking on the credit now. I do see the $40 credit was approved on May 28 - it may still be processing on your bank's end. I don't see a record of a scheduled callback, unfortunately.

records.CommentBody: Customer states bank shows no pending credit and is considering canceling after a missed callback. Escalating to senior specialist to resolve both the credit and the missed callback. Handing off now.
